seo.title: 100 Authentication & Authorization resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical Authentication & Authorization resources for engineers.

content.intro: This guide provides a technical roadmap for implementing authentication and authorization in modern full-stack applications. It covers provider selection, multi-tenant architecture, and security hardening patterns for developers building SaaS and enterprise-grade systems.

items.title: Clerk User Management
items.description: Use Clerk for rapid Next.js integration. It provides pre-built <SignIn /> components and a middleware-based session check that handles multi-session management out of the box.

items.title: Auth.js (formerly NextAuth.js)
items.description: Implement for projects requiring open-source flexibility. Use the 'Database Session' strategy with the Prisma Adapter to maintain full control over user data in your own PostgreSQL instance.

items.title: Lucia Auth
items.description: A database-agnostic library that handles session management without a heavy framework. Best for developers who want to manage their own database schemas while avoiding the complexities of low-level crypto.

items.title: Supabase Auth with RLS
items.description: Leverage Supabase's built-in GoTrue server. Use PostgreSQL Row Level Security (RLS) to automatically filter data based on the `auth.uid()` of the requesting user.

items.title: WorkOS for Enterprise SSO
items.description: Integrate WorkOS when moving upmarket. It abstracts complex SAML and SCIM integrations into a single API, allowing connection to providers like Okta and Azure AD.

items.title: Firebase Auth
items.description: Utilize for high-scale mobile and web apps. Use the Admin SDK on the backend to verify ID tokens and manage custom user claims for basic role-based access.

items.title: Ory Kratos
items.description: Deploy Kratos for a self-hosted, cloud-native identity server. It separates identity management from the UI, requiring you to build your own login/registration flows via API.

items.title: Keycloak for On-Premise
items.description: A robust Java-based solution for enterprise environments. Supports OIDC, SAML, and Social Login with extensive management consoles for complex LDAP integrations.

items.title: Passport.js for Express
items.description: The legacy standard for Node.js. Use specific strategies like `passport-jwt` or `passport-google-oauth20` to modularize authentication logic in monolithic architectures.

items.title: Zitadel for Multi-Tenancy
items.description: An open-source identity management system built specifically for multi-tenancy. Use its 'Projects' and 'Roles' features to manage access across different customer organizations.

items.title: CASL for Isomorphic Permissions
items.description: Define permissions in a central `ability.ts` file using CASL. This allows you to use the same logic for UI toggles and backend API enforcement.

items.title: Permit.io for Policy-as-Code
items.description: Externalize authorization logic using Permit.io. It provides a UI for non-developers to manage roles while syncing policies to your local OPA (Open Policy Agent) instance.

items.title: Cerbos for Decoupled Authz
items.description: Run Cerbos as a sidecar or service. Define stateless policies in YAML to handle complex context-aware permissions without bloating your application code.

items.title: Stripe-Based Gatekeeping
items.description: Sync Stripe subscription status to your user profiles. Implement middleware that checks the `subscription_status` field before allowing access to premium features or API endpoints.

items.title: Hierarchical RBAC in Prisma
items.description: Model roles using an Enum and a join table for permissions. Use Prisma's `$use` middleware to automatically inject tenant IDs into every query for data isolation.

items.title: Oso for Relationship-Based Access
items.description: Use Oso's Polar language to define 'ReBAC' (Relationship-Based Access Control). Ideal for 'Folder > Document' style permissions where access is inherited.

items.title: Clerk Organizations API
items.description: Use the Organizations feature to handle B2B tenant switching. Developers can use the `useOrganization` hook to fetch context-specific data without manual filtering.

items.title: Auth.js Session Callbacks
items.description: Customize the `session` and `jwt` callbacks in Auth.js config to include the `tenantId` or `role` in the client-side session object for immediate UI updates.

items.title: PostgreSQL Schema Isolation
items.description: For high-compliance apps, use separate DB schemas per tenant. Implement a connection pooler that selects the schema based on the user's authenticated `org_id`.

items.title: ABAC with User Metadata
items.description: Implement Attribute-Based Access Control by checking user metadata (e.g., department, location) against resource attributes during the request lifecycle.

items.title: Refresh Token Rotation
items.description: Implement rotation logic where every time a new access token is requested, the old refresh token is invalidated. This mitigates the risk of stolen long-lived tokens.

items.title: PKCE for OAuth2 Flows
items.description: Enforce Proof Key for Code Exchange (PKCE) for all OAuth flows, including server-side, to prevent authorization code injection attacks.

items.title: Upstash Rate Limiting
items.description: Protect auth endpoints (login, forgot password) using Upstash Redis and `@upstash/ratelimit` to prevent brute-force attacks in serverless environments.

items.title: HTTP-Only Secure Cookies
items.description: Store session IDs or JWTs in cookies with `HttpOnly`, `Secure`, and `SameSite=Lax` flags to prevent XSS-based token theft.

items.title: WebAuthn / Passkeys
items.description: Integrate SimpleWebAuthn to allow users to authenticate using biometric hardware, significantly reducing reliance on vulnerable passwords.

items.title: Iron Session for Stateless Auth
items.description: Use `iron-session` for encrypted, stateless, and signed cookies. It provides a session utility that works across Next.js API routes and Server Components.

items.title: JWT Claims Validation
items.description: Always validate the `iss` (issuer), `aud` (audience), and `exp` (expiration) claims using a library like `jose` when receiving tokens from external providers.

items.title: Helmet.js Security Headers
items.description: Apply Helmet.js to your Express or Fastify app to set headers like `Content-Security-Policy` and `X-Frame-Options` to prevent clickjacking and injection.

items.title: TOTP 2FA with Otplib
items.description: Add multi-factor authentication using `otplib` to generate and verify TOTP codes. Store the encrypted secret in your database, never in plain text.

items.title: AI Endpoint Rate Limiting
items.description: Use per-user usage tracking in your auth middleware to limit OpenAI/Anthropic API calls, preventing a single authenticated user from draining your credits.

pro_tips: Never store JWTs in localStorage; it makes your application vulnerable to XSS token theft. Use HTTP-only cookies instead.
Implement a 'Global Sign Out' by maintaining a 'last_logout_at' timestamp in your database and comparing it against the JWT 'issued_at' (iat) claim.
Use a dedicated 'Auth' database or schema to keep identity data separate from application data, simplifying security audits and GDPR compliance.
Always implement a 'Shadow User' record in your primary database when using 3rd-party providers (Clerk/Auth0) to link internal data via a stable UUID.
Set your Access Token TTL (Time To Live) to a short duration (e.g., 15 minutes) and use Refresh Tokens to maintain the session securely.
